meaning: n.诸侯,封臣,附庸,诸侯adj.为臣的,仆从的
examples: The historical prologue, thirdly, explains why Israel accepts her place in the suzerain-vassal relationship.
The romantic town of Rovinj a former Venetian vassal state rises from the Adriatic like an estranged island of Venice.
After a few years Fuchai let him return home as his vassal.
Some other vassal would come along and spear his fellow-knight in the name of love.
Yet Mr Yanukovich knows that a turn to Russia could turn him into Moscow's vassal, even if it would earn him a few extra votes.
